etymology.description: 肖(肖) (xiào, 'resemble') combines with 像(像) (xiàng, 'image') to form the word 肖像, which means 'portrait'.
etymology.interpretation: 肖(肖) (xiào, 'resemble') conveys likeness or similarity, which gives the idea of something being similar in appearance. 像(像) (xiàng, 'image') suggests a visual representation of an object or person. Thus, 肖像 involves creating a visual representation ('image') that captures the likeness ('resemblance') of a person, leading directly to the meaning of 'portrait'.

examples.chinese: 壁炉上挂着一幅精美的肖像(壁爐上掛著一幅精美的肖像)。
examples.pinyin: Bìlú shàng guàzhe yī fú jīngměi de xiàoxiàng.
examples.english: A beautiful portrait hangs above the fireplace.

examples.chinese: 他为艺术展准备了几幅肖像(他為藝術展準備了幾幅肖像)。
examples.pinyin: Tā wèi yìshù zhǎn zhǔnbèile jǐ fú xiàoxiàng.
examples.english: He prepared several portraits for the art exhibition.

examples.chinese: 这幅肖像生动地展现了她的个性(這幅肖像生動地展現了她的個性)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè fú xiàoxiàng shēngdòng de zhǎnxiànle tā de gèxìng.
examples.english: This portrait vividly showcases her personality.
